title: Split WAIT and RETAIN dependency semantics (#1375)
goal: Implement independent WAIT and RETAIN dependency semantics in tensormap_and_ringbuffer so readiness can be reduced without shortening tensor lifetime.
context: Use GitHub Issue #1375 as the canonical scope. Implement on current main rather than cherry-picking feat/two-kinds-of-dep; preserve manual-scope safety, generation pinning, and DFX provenance.
Keep a2a3 and a5 behavior aligned.

acceptance_criteria.description: WAIT-only, RETAIN-only, and WAIT|RETAIN edges behave correctly through submit, wiring, completion, release, inline storage, spill storage, and the all-producers-completed path.

verification.value: cmake -B tests/ut/cpp/build -S tests/ut/cpp && cmake --build tests/ut/cpp/build && ctest --test-dir tests/ut/cpp/build -LE requires_hardware --output-on-failure

acceptance_criteria.description: Creator outputs remain retained until the consumer releases them, while WAIT-only producers can be reclaimed without waiting for consumer completion; duplicate sources preserve the stronger flags.
a2a3 and a5 implementations plus dependency replay expose the same edge semantics and preserve flags/provenance.
